metadata.name: Interpret and Manage Syslog Events
metadata.description: Configure the syslog log file to redirect all the log messages of a specified priority to a log file
objectives: Configure the syslog log file to use the rsyslog service to write all log messages with the debug priority to a log file

tasks.name: Verify debug.conf configuration file exists
tasks.description: The /etc/rsyslog.d/debug.conf file must exist with rsyslog rules
tasks.failure_message: The debug.conf configuration file is missing

tasks.name: Verify debug logging rule is configured
tasks.description: The debug.conf must contain the rule to log debug messages to /var/log/messages-debug
tasks.success_message: Debug logging rule is correctly configured
tasks.failure_message: Debug logging rule is not correctly configured
hints: This logs all facilities at debug priority or higher

tasks.name: Verify rsyslog service is running
tasks.description: The rsyslog service must be running for logging to work
tasks.failure_message: The rsyslog service is not running
hints: Restart rsyslog: sudo systemctl restart rsyslog

tasks.name: Verify debug log file exists
tasks.description: The /var/log/messages-debug file should exist after rsyslog restart
tasks.failure_message: The debug log file does not exist
hints: Restart rsyslog to create the file: sudo systemctl restart rsyslog
Generate a test message: logger -p user.debug 'Test message'

tasks.name: Verify debug messages are being logged
tasks.failure_message: Debug messages are not being logged
hints: Ensure rsyslog was restarted after creating debug.conf
Test with: logger -p user.debug 'Debug Message Test'
